name: LEAP Legal Software API
what_it_does: LEAP cloud legal practice management REST API for small to mid-size law firms covering matter management, time billing, document automation, trust accounting, and client portal. Enables AI agents to manage matter and client setup for automated legal matter intake workflow, handle time entry and billing for attorney productivity automation, access document automation and precedent generation for legal document drafting automation, retrieve trust accounting and client ledger for IOLTA compliance automation, manage invoice generation and accounts receivable for billing cycle automation, handle calendar and court deadline management for litigation docket automation, access client portal and communication for client relationship automation, retrieve file and document management for matter document control, manage matter workflow and task assignment for legal process automation, and integrate LEAP with court filing systems, e-signature (DocuSign), accounting (Xero, QuickBooks), and legal research platforms.
use_cases: Capturing attorney time from productivity agents
Generating legal documents from drafting agents
Managing trust accounts from IOLTA compliance agents
Processing invoices from billing automation agents
not_for: Large law firms needing enterprise scale (use Elite 3E or Aderant)
Government and court case management systems
best_when: A small to mid-size law firm in Australia, UK, Ireland, or North America wanting AI agents to automate matter management, document drafting, trust accounting, billing, and client communication within LEAP's cloud-native legal practice management platform.
avoid_when: IOLTA TRUST ACCOUNTING STATE/JURISDICTION BAR COMPLIANCE: Automated trust fund transactions via LEAP must comply with jurisdiction-specific bar rules for IOLTA accounts (Australia: Law Society trust accounting rules; UK: SRA accounts rules; US: state bar IOLTA rules); automated trust disbursement without attorney authorization creates bar regulatory and malpractice exposure. AI DOCUMENT DRAFTING LEGAL REVIEW MANDATORY: Automated legal document generation from LEAP precedents using AI must be reviewed by supervising attorney before filing or delivery; AI-drafted legal documents without attorney review create professional responsibility and malpractice risk. COURT DEADLINE CALENDAR ACCURACY: Automated docket management from LEAP court deadline calendar must be verified against court rules for local rules and holiday adjustments; automated deadline calculation errors create statute of limitations and procedural default malpractice. Client communications privilege — automated client communication logging via LEAP client portal must be protected under attorney-client privilege; automated forwarding of client communications outside the matter creates privilege waiver risk.

auth.notes: LEAP uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. Sydney, Australia HQ with offices in UK, Ireland, USA, and Canada. Founded 1992 in Australia. Private company. 70,000+ lawyers globally using LEAP. Strong in Australia, UK, Ireland, and growing in North America. Cloud-native legal practice management for small to mid-size firms. Matter management, document automation, trust accounting, mobile app. Competes with Clio, Smokeball, and MyCase for small law firm practice management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-fee-earner monthly or annual subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-fee-earner (attorney) monthly or annual subscription; all features included in single tier
pricing.notes: Sydney Australia. Private company, founded 1992. 70,000+ lawyers globally. Strong in AU/UK/IE/US. Per-fee-earner subscription, all-inclusive pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client with scopes; billing and trust API throttled for data integrity

known_agent_gotchas: IOLTA TRUST ACCOUNTING JURISDICTION-SPECIFIC BAR RULES: LEAP trust accounting must be configured per jurisdiction bar rules; Australian Law Society rules, SRA accounts rules (UK), and US state bar IOLTA rules differ significantly; automated trust fund transactions must implement jurisdiction-specific authorization and reconciliation workflows; one-size-fits-all trust automation creates bar rule violation

DOCUMENT AUTOMATION PRECEDENT JURISDICTION MAPPING: LEAP document automation uses jurisdiction-specific precedent libraries; automated document generation must select correct jurisdiction precedent; automated use of incorrect jurisdiction precedent (e.g., NSW precedent for VIC matter) creates legally deficient documents

COURT DEADLINE AUTOMATION JURISDICTION VERIFICATION: Automated court deadline calculation from LEAP must account for jurisdiction-specific court rules, local rules, and public holiday variations; LEAP calendar automation without local rule verification creates statute of limitations and filing deadline malpractice risk

Attorney supervision of AI-drafted documents — automated legal document generation using LEAP precedent plus AI augmentation must be reviewed by supervising attorney before filing or client delivery; AI-drafted legal documents without review create professional responsibility violation and legal malpractice exposure

Multi-jurisdiction firm trust account separation — LEAP for multi-jurisdiction law firms (AU + UK) requires separate trust accounts per jurisdiction; automated trust transfers between jurisdictions require legal compliance review; automated inter-jurisdiction trust movement creates regulatory exposure

Client portal message privilege protection — automated client communications logged via LEAP client portal are subject to attorney-client privilege; automated workflow that copies client messages to external CRM or non-privileged system creates privilege waiver; keep all client communications within LEAP matter context
agent_readiness.security_notes: Legal practice management. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2. AU/UK/US/IE. Attorney billing, trust account, and client matter data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Legal practice management. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2. AU/UK/US/IE. Client matter and trust account data.
